scope.shape: standard (meta + entries/messages/list sections; literal titles; two of three carry inline-object entries)

necessityProof.blockerResolved: The last three pure-standard-shape managed-audit sandbox-endpoint credential-resolver upstream-echo-verification renderers still hand-built the same title/meta/section Markdown frame.
necessityProof.laterConsumer: N1 final acceptance can classify the upstream-echo-verification / disabled-precheck / test-only-shell reports as builder-backed.
necessityProof.reuseDecision: All three are standard shape; inline-object entries map directly to the builder's entries section; titles are literals.
necessityProof.growthStopCondition: No new helper added. A full shape census confirmed this batch empties the 'pure-standard with tests' subset; the remaining 112 unmigrated renderers all contain for-loops, h3 (### ) sub-sections, or .map/.flatMap dynamic assembly and are deferred.

shapeCensusAtBatchStart.note: Buckets overlap where a file has multiple traits; pureStandardWithTests is the disjoint safe subset migrated in this batch.

focusedRendererBatch.command: npx vitest run <3 renderer tests> test/governanceGrowthRatchet.test.ts

byteIdentical.method: The three tests assert Markdown via toContain (not full-string equality), so a throwaway harness rendered each report before and after the edit and diffed them. The only difference in all three was the runtime-varying '- Generated at:' timestamp line; every other line (including all inline-object entries) was byte-identical. Harness and .tmp dumps were deleted before commit.
